title: 10 best herbal tinctures for dizziness
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for dizziness are concentrated liquid extracts of plants that are used as an alternative or complementary treatment for dizziness. They are made by soaking plant material in a mixture of water and alcohol for several weeks, then straining and preserving the resulting liquid. Some herbs commonly used in tinctures for dizziness include ginger, turmeric, and black cohosh. Ginger, for example, has been shown to help reduce symptoms of motion sickness and vertigo, while turmeric has anti-inflammatory properties that may help with inner ear issues. Black cohosh, on the other hand, is thought to help regulate blood pressure and improve circulation. These herbal tinctures can provide a natural and effective way to manage dizziness, improving the overall quality of life for many people. For instance, a person who experiences frequent dizziness due to motion sickness or inner ear issues may find relief by taking a ginger or turmeric tincture before travel or at the onset of symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sweet flag tincture helps with dizziness because it contains active compounds that have been shown to improve circulation and reduce inflammation. These properties can help alleviate symptoms of dizziness by promoting healthy blood flow to the brain and reducing any swelling or irritation in the inner ear, which can contribute to feelings of disorientation and unsteadiness. Additionally, sweet flag has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments related to the nervous system, making it a potentially effective remedy for dizziness caused by anxiety or stress. Overall, sweet flag tincture may provide relief for those experiencing dizziness by addressing some of the underlying causes of this symptom.
remedy_properties: Anticholinergic property: This property helps regulate the activity of the vestibular system, which is important for maintaining balance and reducing dizziness. The active constituents responsible for this property include asarone and bisabolene.

Anxiolytic property: This property helps reduce anxiety and stress, which can often trigger or worsen dizziness. The active constituents responsible for this property include asarone, α-pinene, and β-pinene.

Nausea-reducing property: This property helps alleviate nausea and vomiting, which are common symptoms associated with dizziness. The active constituents responsible for this property include asarone, eugenol, and methyl eugenol.

Circulation-improving property: This property helps improve blood flow and oxygenation to the brain, which can help reduce dizziness caused by poor circulation. The active constituents responsible for this property include camphene, cineole, and phellandrene.

Anti-inflammatory property: This property helps reduce inflammation in the inner ear and nervous system, which can help alleviate dizziness caused by conditions such as Meniere's disease. The active constituents responsible for this property include asarone, eugenol, and methyl eugenol.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of fresh sweet flag (Acorus calamus) roots, cleaning them thoroughly and chopping into small pieces.
Combine chopped roots with 1 pint of 80 proof vodka in a glass jar, ensuring the roots are fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking the mixture daily.
Strain the tincture using cheesecloth, discarding the plant material and storing the liquid in a tightly sealed glass container.
Take 1-2 mL of the sweet flag tincture up to three times a day for dizziness, as needed.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: The rhizomes of sweet flag are commonly used to make medicinal tinctures for dizziness due to their calming and sedative properties.
Leaves: The leaves of sweet flag are sometimes used in tinctures for their aromatic and stimulating effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of dizziness.
Flowers: The flowers of sweet flag are used less frequently than the rhizomes and leaves, but can be included in tinctures for their mildly uplifting and clarifying effects.
Seeds: The seeds of sweet flag are not typically used in tinctures for dizziness, but may be included for their potential to support digestion and reduce inflammation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood tincture helps with dizziness because it contains a compound called artemisinin, which has been shown to improve circulation and reduce inflammation in the inner ear. This can help to alleviate the symptoms of dizziness and vertigo. Additionally, wormwood has been used traditionally as a digestive aid, and can help to reduce nausea and vomiting that may accompany dizziness. The herb is also known to have calming effects on the nervous system, which can help to reduce anxiety and promote a sense of balance and stability. Overall, wormwood tincture can be a helpful natural remedy for those experiencing dizziness.
remedy_properties: Anticholinergic property: Wormwood tincture's anticholinergic properties help regulate the inner ear's balance, reducing dizziness caused by vestibular dysfunction. The active constituent responsible for this property is primarily the alkaloid, thujone.

Anti-inflammatory property: Wormwood tincture's anti-inflammatory effects help reduce inflammation in the inner ear and surrounding tissues, alleviating dizziness and vertigo symptoms. Sesquiterpene lactones, such as parthenolide, contribute to this property.

Anxiolytic property: Wormwood tincture's anxiolytic properties help manage anxiety and stress related to dizziness and vertigo, providing a calming effect on the nervous system. Flavonoids and essential oils, such as chamazulene, are responsible for this property.

Circulation-stimulating property: Wormwood tincture's ability to improve blood circulation helps ensure proper oxygen and nutrient supply to the inner ear and brain, reducing dizziness caused by insufficient blood flow. The active constituents responsible for this property include the essential oil, 1,8-cineole, and various flavonoids.

Nausea-reducing property: Wormwood tincture's ability to alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with dizziness and vertigo helps improve overall well-being. The active constituent primarily responsible for this property is the alkaloid, thujone.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of dried wormwood herb into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 1 pint of vodka or grain alcohol (80-100 proof) over the herbs, ensuring they are fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out the liquid and discarding the solid material.
Store the final tincture in a tightly sealed glass bottle, away from light. Adults may take 1-2 ml up to three times a day for dizziness. Consult with a healthcare provider before use.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain compounds that may help reduce inflammation and improve circulation, potentially alleviating symptoms of dizziness.
Flowers: Rich in medicinal properties, including antioxidants and anti-inflammatory agents, which may help stabilize inner ear functions and reduce dizziness.
Seeds: Packed with essential oils and active compounds, such as thujone, which may help stimulate the central nervous system and alleviate symptoms of vertigo and dizziness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cinnamon tincture helps with dizziness because it contains active compounds that can help regulate blood sugar levels and improve circulation. Dizziness can be a symptom of low blood sugar or poor circulation, and cinnamon has been shown to help stabilize blood sugar levels and improve blood flow. Additionally, cinnamon has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the inner ear, which can also contribute to dizziness. By addressing these underlying causes, cinnamon tincture can provide relief from dizziness and promote overall balance and stability.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Cinnamon tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation in the inner ear, a common cause of dizziness, due to the presence of compounds like cinnamaldehyde and eugenol.
Antiemetic: The antiemetic properties of cinnamon tincture help alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with dizziness, thanks to the presence of active constituents like cinnamaldehyde and tannins.

Circulatory stimulant: Cinnamon tincture stimulates blood flow and circulation, which can help reduce dizziness caused by poor circulation or hypotension, due to the presence of compounds like cinnamaldehyde and coumarin.
Analgesic: The analgesic properties of cinnamon tincture help relieve headaches and other pain associated with dizziness, due to the presence of active constituents like cinnamaldehyde and eugenol.

Antioxidant: Cinnamon tincture's antioxidant properties help protect the body from oxidative stress and damage, which can contribute to dizziness and other symptoms, thanks to the presence of compounds like polyphenols, flavonoids, and phenolic acids.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 cup of dried cinnamon bark into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 2 cups of vodka or rum with at least 40% alcohol content over the cinnamon, ensuring it is fully submerged.
Seal the jar tightly and store in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
Strain the mixture using a cheesecloth or fine mesh strainer, squeezing out the liquid and discarding the solids.
Store the final tincture in a clean, amber glass bottle, labeling it with the ingredients, date, and dosage instructions (typically 1-2 mL up to three times a day).
remedy_parts: Bark: The bark of the cinnamon tree is commonly used in making medicinal tinctures for dizziness due to its ability to improve circulation and stabilize blood sugar levels.
Leaves: The leaves of the cinnamon plant are used for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of dizziness caused by inflammation or oxidative stress.
Seeds: Cinnamon seeds contain essential oils and compounds that can help reduce nausea and improve digestion, making them useful for treating dizziness caused by digestive issues.

Stems: The stems, or rhizomes, of the cinnamon plant are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory effects, making them a popular choice for treating dizziness caused by free radical damage or inflammation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lily of the valley tincture helps with dizziness because it contains natural compounds that have a positive effect on the circulatory system. This herb is known to improve blood flow, particularly to the brain, which can help alleviate symptoms of dizziness. Additionally, it contains cardiac glycosides, which can help regulate heart function and improve overall cardiovascular health. This can be especially beneficial for individuals who experience dizziness due to issues related to their heart or circulatory system. Furthermore, the tincture's calming properties can help reduce anxiety and stress, which can also contribute to feelings of dizziness.
remedy_properties: Anti-arrhythmic property: Lily of the valley tincture helps regulate heart rate and rhythm, which is important for dizziness caused by irregular heartbeat or low blood pressure, due to its cardiac glycosides such as convallotoxin.
Diuretic property: It promotes urine production, which helps lower blood pressure and reduce fluid retention, alleviating dizziness from hypertension or edema, thanks to its saponins and flavonoids.

Vasodilatory property: Lily of the valley tincture widens blood vessels, improving blood flow and oxygen supply to the brain, reducing dizziness from insufficient blood supply, attributed to its glycosides and saponins.
Nervine property: It has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce anxiety and stress-related dizziness, due to its alkaloids and essential oils.
Anticholinergic property: Lily of the valley tincture can help balance neurotransmitters, reducing vertigo and other inner ear issues, owing to its alkaloids like convallamarine and convallotidine.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lily of the valley flowers, ensuring they are fully open, and gently rinse them to remove any dirt or debris.
Fill a clean glass jar with the harvested flowers, filling it three-quarters of the way, then pour 80-proof vodka over the flowers until they are completely submerged.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for two to four weeks, shaking it gently every few days to help the extraction process.
After the specified time, strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine-mesh strainer, discarding the spent flowers and squeezing out any remaining liquid.

Transfer the resulting tincture to a clean, dark glass bottle, label it with the ingredients, preparation date, and recommended dosage, and store it in a cool, dark place for up to one year. Recommended dosage for dizziness is 1-2 mL, three times a day.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain anti-inflammatory and vasodilatory properties, which can help improve blood flow to the brain and reduce dizziness.
Rhyzomes: Have diuretic and sedative effects, which can help regulate fluid balance and reduce anxiety related to dizziness.
Leaves: Contain antioxidants and flavonoids, which can help protect the inner ear from damage and improve balance.
Flowers: Contain essential oils with antispasmodic and relaxant properties, which can help alleviate muscle tension and reduce dizziness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yanhusuo tincture helps with dizziness because it contains the active compound, dihydropyridine, which is believed to improve blood flow to the brain and inner ear. This increased circulation can help alleviate symptoms of dizziness and vertigo. Additionally, yanhusuo has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries to treat a variety of ailments, including circulation problems and anxiety, both of which can contribute to feelings of dizziness. The herb's calming effects may also help reduce anxiety and promote a sense of balance and stability. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare provider before using yanhusuo tincture or any other herbal remedy.
remedy_properties: Anti-vertigo: Yanhusuo tincture contains alkaloids such as corydaline and tetrahydropalmatine, which help alleviate vertigo and dizziness by regulating the vestibular system and improving blood flow to the brain.
Anxiolytic: The tincture has calming effects due to its alkaloid content, reducing anxiety and nervous tension that can contribute to dizziness.
Analgesic: Yanhusuo tincture contains pain-relieving compounds like tetrahydropalmatine, which can help manage headaches or other pain-related dizziness.
Vasodilatory: The tincture can help widen blood vessels, increasing blood flow to the brain and reducing dizziness caused by poor circulation or ischemia.
Antioxidant: Yanhusuo contains antioxidant compounds that protect brain cells from oxidative stress and damage, promoting overall brain health and reducing dizziness caused by neurological issues.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 ounce of dried Yanhusuo root into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 2 cups of 80 proof vodka or grain alcohol over the herbs, ensuring they are fully submerged.
Seal the jar tightly and store in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out any excess liquid.
Store the final tincture in a clean, dark glass bottle and use as needed for dizziness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Yanhusuo roots are commonly used in medicinal tinctures for dizziness due to their anti-vertigo properties, which help to alleviate symptoms of dizziness and lightheadedness.
Rhizomes: Yanhusuo rhizomes have been used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat dizziness, as they contain active compounds that improve blood flow and reduce symptoms of vertigo.
Seeds: Yanhusuo seeds are used in medicinal tinctures for dizziness due to their sedative effects, which can help to calm the mind and reduce symptoms of anxiety and stress-related dizziness.

Bark: Yanhusuo bark is sometimes used in medicinal tinctures for dizziness due to its ability to improve circulation and reduce inflammation, which can help to alleviate symptoms of vertigo and lightheadedness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tincture helps with dizziness because it contains active compounds that are known to improve circulation and reduce inflammation. These properties can help alleviate symptoms of dizziness by promoting healthy blood flow to the brain and reducing any swelling or pressure in the head. Additionally, the tincture may help to calm the nervous system, which can help to reduce feelings of anxiety or stress that can contribute to dizziness. By addressing these underlying factors, maidenhair tree tincture can provide relief from dizziness and promote overall balance and stability.
remedy_properties: Anti-vertigo: Maidenhair tree tincture helps reduce dizziness by alleviating symptoms of vertigo, thanks to its active constituents like flavonoids and essential oils.
Vasodilatory: The tincture can help dilate blood vessels, improving circulation and reducing dizziness caused by poor blood flow, due to the presence of prostaglandins.
Anxiolytic: Maidenhair tree tincture has calming properties that can help reduce anxiety-induced dizziness, as it contains compounds like magnolol and honokiol that interact with GABA receptors.

Anti-inflammatory: The tincture can help reduce inflammation in the inner ear, a common cause of dizziness, by inhibiting the release of pro-inflammatory cytokines, thanks to its active constituents like mangiferin.

Antioxidant: Maidenhair tree tincture can help protect cells from oxidative damage, which may contribute to dizziness, by scavenging free radicals, due to its high content of polyphenols and other antioxidant compounds.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 handfuls of fresh maidenhair tree fern leaves and stems, ensuring they are free from pesticides and pollutants.
Chop the leaves and stems into small pieces, then place them in a clean, dry glass jar, filling it up to three-quarters capacity.
Pour 100-proof vodka or pure grain alcohol into the jar, ensuring the plant material is completely submerged, and leave a 1-inch space at the top.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.

After the allotted time, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or muslin into a dark glass bottle, squeezing out any excess liquid, and store it in a cool, dark place. Take 1-2 mL up to three times daily for dizziness.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain high levels of vitamins and antioxidants, making them effective in alleviating dizziness caused by oxidative stress.
Stems: Rich in flavonoids and tannins, stems can help improve circulation and reduce symptoms of dizziness.
Roots: Known for their anti-inflammatory properties, roots can help reduce inner ear inflammation, a common cause of dizziness.
Bark: Contains essential oils and alkaloids that can help regulate blood pressure, reducing episodes of dizziness.
Seeds: High in fatty acids and antioxidants, seeds can help protect nerve cells and improve balance, reducing feelings of dizziness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Korean ginseng tincture helps with dizziness because it contains active compounds like ginsenosides, which are known to improve circulation and boost energy levels. By promoting better blood flow to the brain, it helps alleviate symptoms of dizziness. Additionally, ginseng has adaptogenic properties that help the body manage stress and anxiety, common triggers of dizziness. Its antioxidant effects also protect cells from damage, further supporting overall health and well-being. Therefore, incorporating Korean ginseng tincture into your daily routine may help reduce feelings of dizziness and improve your quality of life.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic: Korean ginseng tincture helps the body adapt to stress and fatigue, which can contribute to dizziness by maintaining homeostasis and improving overall health. Active constituents include ginsenosides and polysaccharides.

Vasoregulatory: Korean ginseng tincture helps regulate blood flow and blood pressure, which can help reduce dizziness caused by poor circulation or low blood pressure. Active constituents include ginsenosides and volatile oils.

Nervine: Korean ginseng tincture has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate dizziness caused by anxiety or nervous tension. Active constituents include ginsenosides and eleutherosides.

Anti-inflammatory: Korean ginseng tincture reduces inflammation in the body, which can help alleviate dizziness caused by inflammation or swelling in the inner ear or brain. Active constituents include ginsenosides and phenolic compounds.

Antioxidant: Korean ginseng tincture acts as an antioxidant, protecting cells from damage and reducing oxidative stress, which can contribute to dizziness. Active constituents include ginsenosides and polyphenols.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 ounce of Korean ginseng root into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Add 1 cup of vodka or pure grain alcohol (80-100 proof) and 1 cup of distilled water to the jar.
Tightly seal the jar and shake well to ensure the ginseng is fully submerged in the liquid.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it once a day.
After 4-6 weeks, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle and store in a cool, dark place. Take 1-2 mL up to three times a day as needed for dizziness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Korean ginseng are the most commonly used part of the plant in making medicinal tinctures for dizziness due to their high concentration of active compounds, such as ginsenosides, which help to improve circulation and reduce inflammation.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Korean ginseng are used in medicinal tinctures for dizziness because they contain adaptogenic properties that help to reduce stress and anxiety, which can contribute to feelings of dizziness.

Seeds: The seeds of Korean ginseng are used in medicinal tinctures for dizziness due to their high concentration of antioxidants, which help to protect the body from damage caused by free radicals and improve overall health.

Leaves: The leaves of Korean ginseng are used in medicinal tinctures for dizziness because they contain compounds that help to improve cognitive function and reduce inflammation, which can contribute to feelings of dizziness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Firmoss tincture helps with dizziness because it contains natural ingredients that have been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of vertigo and inner ear disturbances. The primary active compound in firmoss, betahistine, is believed to work by improving blood flow to the inner ear and reducing fluid build-up, which can contribute to feelings of dizziness. Additionally, firmoss tincture may help to regulate the vestibular system, which is responsible for maintaining balance and spatial orientation. By promoting overall ear and balance health, firmoss tincture can help to reduce the frequency and severity of dizziness episodes, providing relief for those who struggle with this debilitating symptom.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Firmoss tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling in the inner ear, which can cause dizziness and vertigo. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids and terpenoids.

Anticholinergic: Firmoss tincture has anticholinergic properties that help regulate the activity of the vestibular system, reducing dizziness and improving balance. The active constituents responsible for this property include alkaloids such as lobeline.

Circulatory stimulant: Firmoss tincture stimulates blood flow to the brain, improving oxygenation and reducing dizziness. The active constituents responsible for this property include essential oils such as eucalyptol and pinene.

Anxiolytic: Firmoss tincture has anxiolytic properties that help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, which can help alleviate dizziness caused by stress and anxiety. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids such as luteolin and apigenin.

Analgesic: Firmoss tincture's analgesic properties help relieve pain associated with dizziness and vertigo. The active constituents responsible for this property include alkaloids such as lobeline and terpenoids such as bisabolol.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of fresh Firmoss (Lycopodium clavatum) and rinse it gently to remove any dirt or debris.
Coarsely chop the Firmoss and place it in a clean, dry glass jar, then add 10 ounces of 80-proof vodka or grain alcohol.
Securely seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for two to four weeks, shaking the mixture daily.
After the maceration period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or muslin cloth, squeezing out the excess liquid from the marc (plant material).
Store the final Firmoss tincture in a tightly sealed glass container, away from light, and use 1-2 mL (30-60 drops) up to three times a day for dizziness relief.
remedy_parts: Roots: Firmoss roots are often used in medicinal tinctures for dizziness due to their natural calming and grounding properties.
Leaves: The leaves of the firmoss plant are used to make tinctures for dizziness because they contain compounds that help regulate blood pressure and promote circulation.
Flowers: Firmoss flowers are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them a valuable addition to tinctures for dizziness.
Seeds: The seeds of the firmoss plant are used in tinctures for dizziness because they contain essential oils that can help alleviate symptoms of vertigo and motion sickness.
Bark: The bark of the firmoss plant is used in tinctures for dizziness due to its high concentration of tannins, which can help reduce inflammation and improve circulation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild bugle tincture helps with dizziness because it contains active compounds that have a positive effect on the circulatory system. These compounds can help improve blood flow to the brain, which can reduce feelings of dizziness and lightheadedness. Additionally, wild bugle has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate symptoms of anxiety and stress that can contribute to dizziness. The herb's anti-inflammatory properties may also help reduce inflammation in the inner ear, which can cause balance problems and vertigo. Overall, wild bugle tincture can provide a natural and effective way to manage symptoms of dizziness and improve overall balance and stability.
remedy_properties: Vasodilatory property: Wild bugle tincture helps dilate blood vessels, improving blood flow to the brain, which can alleviate dizziness. The active constituents responsible for this property are the iridoid glycosides, such as aucubin and catalpol.

Anticholinergic property: Wild bugle tincture can help reduce dizziness by regulating the activity of the vestibular system, which is responsible for maintaining balance. The anticholinergic properties come from the alkaloids present in the plant.

Anxiolytic property: Wild bugle tincture has a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation. This can help alleviate symptoms of dizziness caused by anxiety or stress. The flavonoids and triterpenes found in the plant contribute to its anxiolytic properties.

Adaptogenic property: Wild bugle tincture helps the body adapt to stress and fatigue, which can help reduce dizziness caused by physical or mental exhaustion. The active constituents responsible for this property are the phenolic acids and tannins in the plant.

Analgesic property: Wild bugle tincture can help relieve pain associated with dizziness, such as headaches or migraines. The active constituents responsible for this property are the essential oils and terpenoids found in the plant.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of wild bugle aerial parts at peak bloom, then rinse and air dry.
Chop herbs and place in a clean jar, filling it 3/4 full, then add 80 proof vodka until herbs are completely submerged.
Seal jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain tincture using cheesecloth into a dark glass dropper bottle, discarding spent herbs.
Label bottle with ingredients, date, and recommended dosage of 30 drops up to three times daily for dizziness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in medicinal compounds, the wild bugle roots can be used to make tinctures that help reduce dizziness caused by inner ear disturbances.
Leaves: Containing anti-inflammatory and calmative properties, wild bugle leaves can be used to create tinctures that alleviate dizziness related to stress or anxiety.
Flowers: Packed with antioxidants and soothing agents, wild bugle flowers can be used to make tinctures that help reduce dizziness caused by free radical damage or oxidative stress.
Seeds: High in essential oils and nutrients, wild bugle seeds can be used to make tinctures that help improve circulation and reduce dizziness caused by poor blood flow.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tincture helps with dizziness because it contains natural compounds that have a calming effect on the body. It is particularly effective in reducing symptoms of dizziness caused by anxiety or stress. The herb's rosmarinic acid and eugenol content help to regulate the nervous system, promoting relaxation and reducing the likelihood of experiencing dizzy spells. Additionally, lemon balm has been shown to have a positive impact on circulation, which can also contribute to a reduction in feelings of dizziness. Its anti-inflammatory properties may also help to alleviate any inner ear inflammation that could be contributing to dizziness.
remedy_properties: Anticholinergic property: Lemon balm tincture's anticholinergic properties help with dizziness by reducing the overactivity of the vestibular system, which is often responsible for causing dizziness and vertigo symptoms. The active constituents responsible for this property are rosmarinic acid and eugenol.

Anxiolytic property: Lemon balm tincture's anxiolytic properties help with dizziness by reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation, which can help alleviate symptoms of dizziness caused by stress or anxiety disorders. The active constituents responsible for this property are rosmarinic acid and citral.

Sedative property: Lemon balm tincture's sedative properties help with dizziness by promoting sleep and reducing insomnia, which can help alleviate symptoms of dizziness caused by lack of sleep or poor sleep quality. The active constituents responsible for this property are rosmarinic acid and citronellal.

Antispasmodic property: Lemon balm tincture's antispasmodic properties help with dizziness by reducing muscle spasms and tension, which can help alleviate symptoms of dizziness caused by muscle tension or spasms in the neck or back. The active constituents responsible for this property are rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid.

Nervine property: Lemon balm tincture's nervine properties help with dizziness by toning and soothing the nervous system, which can help alleviate symptoms of dizziness caused by nervous system imbalances or disorders. The active constituents responsible for this property are rosmarinic acid and citronellal.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of fresh lemon balm leaves, carefully bruising them to release their oils.
Submerge the leaves in a clean glass jar, adding 10 ounces of vodka or grain alcohol (80-100 proof) to cover the leaves.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for two to four weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out the liquid and discarding the spent herbs.
Store the resulting lemon balm tincture in a tightly sealed glass container, preferably in a cool, dark location; take 1-2 mL up to three times a day for dizziness, as needed.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of lemon balm are commonly used to make medicinal tinctures for dizziness due to their calming and soothing properties.
Flowers: The flowers of lemon balm contain high levels of antioxidants, making them useful for reducing inflammation and preventing dizziness caused by oxidative stress.
Stems: Although less potent than the leaves and flowers, the stems of lemon balm can still be used in tinctures and provide a mild sedative effect to help alleviate dizziness.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat dizziness with herbal tinctures is by using a combination of ginger, turmeric, and black cohosh. Ginger helps alleviate symptoms of vertigo, while turmeric's anti-inflammatory properties reduce inner ear inflammation. Black cohosh is known to aid in balancing hormones, which can be a contributing factor to dizziness. Mix 10-15 drops of each tincture in a small amount of water and consume up to three times a day. Always consult a healthcare provider before starting any new treatment.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to dizziness that are treated with herbal tinctures include vertigo, motion sickness, and anxiety-induced dizziness. Ginkgo Biloba is a popular herbal tincture known to improve circulation in the inner ear, reducing vertigo and dizziness. For motion sickness, options like ginger or peppermint can help alleviate symptoms. Anxiety-related dizziness can be treated with tinctures such as passionflower or valerian root, which have calming and relaxing properties. It is essential to consult a healthcare professional before starting any new herbal treatment.
